mission.title: etcd: etcd client v3 v3.5.0 sends invalid `:authority` header field value
mission.description: etcd client v3 v3.5.0 sends invalid `:authority` header field value. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current etcd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your etcd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
helm list -n etcd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working etcd installation.

steps.title: Review etcd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant etcd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
kubectl get configmap -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=etcd
```
Starting from etcd v3.5.0, etcd client v3 sends invalid `:authority` header field value which violates RFC.

steps.title: Apply the fix for etcd client v3 v3.5.0 sends invalid `:authority` header…
steps.description:
This doesn't fully fix authority in multiple endpoint scenario, but
should at least fixes single endpoint scenario.
```yaml
But now with the v3.5.0, it sends like this:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
kubectl get events -n etcd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "etcd client v3 v3.5.0 sends invalid `:authority` header…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This doesn't fully fix authority in multiple endpoint scenario, but
should at least fixes single endpoint scenario.
codeSnippets: But now with the v3.5.0, it sends like this:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with etcd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
